tool_name: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check

inputs.name: Wall Thickness (t)
inputs.note: Standard: 100 mm block, 140 mm, 190 mm, 200 mm, 215 mm (brick), 250 mm, 300 mm

inputs.name: Masonry Characteristic Compressive Strength (f_k)
inputs.note: EN 1996 Table NA.1: 100 mm dense block + M4 mortar → f_k ≈ 3.5–6 MPa; engineering brick + M12 → 8–15 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Partial Safety Factor for Masonry (γ_M)

options.label: γ_M = 1.5 — Category I units, controlled site
γ_M = 2.0 — Category II units, standard site

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Vertical Load per Unit Length of Wall (N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Load Eccentricity at Mid-Height (e_mid)
inputs.note: EN 1996-1-1 §6.1.2.2: e_mid = e_i + e_hi; e_i = accidental eccentricity = h_eff/450; e_hi from horizontal loads

inputs.name: Wall End / Support Restraint Condition
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: h_eff_m = wall_height_m * end_condition; lambda = h_eff_m * 1000 / wall_thickness_mm  // [m, dimensionless] — Effective Height & Slenderness Ratio (λ) — Ref: EN 1996-1-1:2005 §5.5.1.2 — h_eff = ρ_n × h; EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum λ = 27
fd_mpa = fk_mpa / gamma_M  // [MPa] — Design Compressive Strength (f_d) — Ref: EC6 §6.1.2.1 — f_d = f_k / γ_M; design strength accounting for workmanship and material class

e_rel = eccentricity_mm / wall_thickness_mm; A_phi = 1 - 2 * e_rel; Phi_m = Math.max(A_phi * (1 - (lambda/140)^2), 0.0)  // Capacity Reduction Factor for Slenderness (Φ_m) — Ref: EN 1996-1-1 §6.1.2.2 Eq.(G.2) — Φ_m = (1−2e/t)(1−(λ/140)²); Annex G simplified method
NRd_kn_m = Phi_m * wall_thickness_mm * fd_mpa  // [kN/m] — Design Vertical Load Resistance (N_Rd) — Ref: EC6 §6.1.2.1 — N_Rd = Φ_m × t × f_d (per unit length); t in mm, f_d in MPa → kN/m

DCR = N_Ed_kn_m / NRd_kn_m; e_limit_mm = wall_thickness_mm / 3  // [dimensionless, mm] — Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) & Utilisation — Ref: DCR ≤ 1.0 required; e_limit = t/3 per EC6 §6.1.2.2 — maximum allowable eccentricity
t_req_mm = Math.ceil(N_Ed_kn_m / (Phi_m * fd_mpa))  // [mm] — Minimum Required Wall Thickness for Load (t_req) — Ref: Rearranged from N_Rd: t_req = N_Ed / (Φ_m × f_d); round up to standard block size

rules.message: Wall load capacity exceeded — increase thickness, use higher-strength masonry units, or reduce applied load
Slenderness ratio > 27: EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum limit — wall is too slender; add pilasters, returns, or intermediate lateral supports
Eccentricity exceeds t/3: EC6 §6.1.2.2 limit — Φ_m approaches zero; review structural layout to reduce eccentricity; consider larger wall thickness

smart_warnings.message: High slenderness with pinned ends: Φ_m strongly reduced — adding simple floor/roof tie at top converts to fixed-pin (ρ=0.75), increasing N_Rd by ~25%
Low-strength masonry under significant load: specify minimum M4 mortar and Category I units with testing — avoid using generic f_k without material testing on site
Utilisation > 80%: marginal capacity for concentrated loads (beam bearings) — verify bearing stress at load introduction point per EC6 §6.1.3
Thin wall (< 100 mm) under significant vertical load: structural masonry requires minimum 100 mm; check if wall is load-bearing or partition — reclassify if non-structural
Combined high eccentricity + slenderness: double penalty on Φ_m — capacity is severely reduced; detailed second-order analysis required per EC6 Annex G

fmea.effect: Wall load capacity exceeded — increase thickness, use higher-strength masonry units, or reduce applied load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Slenderness ratio > 27: EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum limit — wall is too slender; add pilasters, returns, or intermediate lateral supports
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Eccentricity exceeds t/3: EC6 §6.1.2.2 limit — Φ_m approaches zero; review structural layout to reduce eccentricity; consider larger wall thickness

fmea.failureMode: High slenderness with pinned ends: Φ_m strongly reduced — adding simple floor/roof tie at top converts to fixed-pin (ρ=0.75), increasing N_Rd by ~25%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda > 20 && end_condition === 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength masonry under significant load: specify minimum M4 mortar and Category I units with testing — avoid using generic f_k without material testing on site
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fk_mpa < 3.5 && N_Ed_kn_m > 100

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 80%: marginal capacity for concentrated loads (beam bearings) — verify bearing stress at load introduction point per EC6 §6.1.3
Thin wall (< 100 mm) under significant vertical load: structural masonry requires minimum 100 mm; check if wall is load-bearing or partition — reclassify if non-structural
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wall_thickness_mm < 100 && N_Ed_kn_m > 50

fmea.failureMode: Combined high eccentricity + slenderness: double penalty on Φ_m — capacity is severely reduced; detailed second-order analysis required per EC6 Annex G
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > wall_thickness_mm * 0.25 && lambda > 18
